expectations.text: Warning callout uses correct syntax: > [!warning] with the blocker message
expectations.evidence: Line 1: '> [!warning] Blocker: API rate limits may delay the Q2 release — needs escalation by EOW' — correct Obsidian callout syntax with title inline

expectations.text: Tip callout uses collapsed syntax: > [!tip]- (with minus for collapsed by default)
expectations.evidence: Line 3: '> [!tip]- How to check current rate limit status' — uses the minus sign for collapsed-by-default syntax

expectations.text: Callout content matches the requested text accurately
expectations.evidence: Warning message matches exactly: 'Blocker: API rate limits may delay the Q2 release — needs escalation by EOW'. Tip content includes the curl command 'curl https://api.internal/status' and references the 'rate_limit_remaining' field.

expectations.text: Does not use incorrect callout delimiters (no triple backtick code blocks instead of > syntax)
expectations.evidence: Both callouts use '> [!type]' syntax throughout. No triple backtick fences are used as callout delimiters. Inline code backticks are used correctly for the curl command and field name.

expectations.text: Provides the content in a form ready to paste or append to the file (e.g., shows the exact markdown)
expectations.evidence: The output is a standalone callouts.md file containing only the two callout blocks, ready to copy and paste directly into an Obsidian note.

suggestions: The warning callout could optionally use a title-only format (no inline title) with the blocker message on the next line — both approaches are valid Obsidian syntax, but the inline title form used here is clean and acceptable.

timing.json does not include a separate executor_duration_seconds field; only total_duration_seconds (30.6s) is available. Consider adding executor_duration_seconds to the timing schema for more granular tracking.
eval_feedback.overall: The agent produced a perfect output: both callouts use correct Obsidian syntax, the warning uses > [!warning] with the full blocker message inline, the tip uses the collapsed > [!tip]- form, and all requested content is accurately represented. The output is minimal and paste-ready. All 5 expectations passed.
